provenance.source_corpus: Establishment of a Vegetation Cover on Tundra Kimberlite Mine Tailings: 2. A Field Study

nodes.name: addition of structure-improving and nutrient-providing amendments

source_spans.text: With the addition of structure-improving and nutrient-providing amendments, plant growth on these kimberlite tailings under ﬁeld conditions was signiﬁcantly improved over unamended tailings material.

nodes.name: plant growth on these kimberlite tailings

source_spans.text: With the addition of structure-improving and nutrient-providing amendments, plant growth on these kimberlite tailings under ﬁeld conditions was signiﬁcantly improved over unamended tailings material.
Sewage sludge signiﬁcantly increased available nitrate at 10- to 20- and 20- to 30-cm depths
Sewage sludge signiﬁcantly increased available nitrate at 10- to 20- and 20- to 30-cm depths
P availability was signiﬁcantly increased with sewage sludge (F ¼ 0.0001) at all depths.
Peat moss signiﬁcantly increased P availability at 0–10 cm ( F ¼ 0.0001) and 20–30 cm ( F ¼ 0.0075)
CEC was signiﬁcantly increased by peat moss at 10–20 cm ( F ¼ 0.0001) and 20–30 cm ( F ¼ 0.0006)
CEC was signiﬁcantly decreased by lake sediment at 10–20 cm ( F ¼ 0.0001) and at 20–30 cm (F ¼ 0.0070).
Organic carbon was signiﬁcantly increased by sewage sludge and peat moss at all depths.
In the ﬁrst ﬁeld season, ground cover (32%) signiﬁcantly increased with sewage sludge ( F ¼ 0.0003), applied in combination with peat moss, gypsum, and rock phosphate
Sewage sludge, peat moss, and lake sediment in combination signiﬁcantly increased plant height (Table 3).
Sewage sludge, peat moss, gypsum, and rock phosphate treatment produced the highest mean shoot biomass (336.2 g/m2)
Peat moss signiﬁcantly improved plant growth over amendment combinations without peat moss (F ¼ 0.0001)
Peat moss performed well, increasing water- and nutrient-holding capacities and overall tailings structure.
Peat moss performed well, increasing water- and nutrient-holding capacities and overall tailings structure.
Sewage sludge was the most effective amendment in nutrient provision
Festuca rubra and Deschampsia beringensis produced the best plant growth response.
Festuca rubra and Deschampsia beringensis produced the best plant growth response.
All seeded native grasses, with the exception of Arctagrostis latifolia , which did not grow on the Ekati tailings
Calcium additions did not signiﬁcantly increase the amount of available calcium in ameliorating the tailings serpentine state.
Calcium additions did not signiﬁcantly increase the amount of available calcium in ameliorating the tailings serpentine state.

nodes.name: Sewage sludge, peat moss, gypsum, and rock phosphate treatment

source_spans.text: Sewage sludge, peat moss, gypsum, and rock phosphate treatment produced the highest mean shoot biomass (336.2 g/m2)
